subject: [Contrib] Add EJBCA Server
commit_message:
[Contrib] Add EJBCA Server

EJBCA Server is used to test that CMPv2 Certificate handling is well
done in ONAP.

Issue-ID: AAF-1083
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: I5e2d25b68b5cd80d3c7bf282ce871dd81e711ff6

first try.

For information:
@Krzysztof, I was unable to use "GenericKV" type with common.secretFast (at best I was able to generate a secret with nothing inside...) so I used 2 secrets of 'password' type

contrib charts is toggled by global.addTestingComponents, which is "false" per default but overrided to true on onap/resources/overrides/onap-all.yaml

ejbca within contrib charts is toggled by global.cmpv2Enabled, which is "true" per default everywhere

guys, any ways to check the values are good?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> guys, any ways to check the values are good?

Besides e2e testing with cert-service and init container with cert-service client, you can enter ejbca container and perform 2 commands
ejbca.sh ca listcas # should list one ca named ManagmentCA
ejbca.sh config cmp listalias # should list 2 aliases

However it will only check if it's configured properly, not if everything works, but I didn't find any better method, than that.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Well, I think I understand why contrib is not deployed and it's because of "deploy.sh" 'infamous' plugin :(

it check only if 'enabled' is set to true for a component.
it doesn't compute the requirements.yaml of onap 😞 and if I do in onap/values.yaml:
```
contrib:
  enabled: '{{ .Values.global.addTestingComponents }}'
```
I don't have true of false when it computes enabled but `'{{ .Values.global.addTestingComponents }}'` so I cannot do this way also...


So I propose an 'ugly' workaround --> use a yaml anchor in values.yaml so contrib.enabled has the same value  than .global.addTestingComponents.

@Krzysztof, your point of view?
